Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:01 ET
ARM soars 14% on CPU news while dealers hold maximum negative gamma
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive dealer short gamma ($-19.9B SPY) creates explosive risk into Core PCE print this morning.
  • 2. ARM's CPU market entry drives rare vol/flow alignment — both calls and puts spiking on breakout momentum.
  • 3. Index puts dominate while single-names see call buying, signaling institutional dispersion hedges ahead of NFP.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma fear

ARM's +14.8% surge on CPU market entry news showcases how negative dealer gamma amplifies breakout moves, with the stock generating both massive call flow (+76% volume) and put activity (+140%) as traders position for continued volatility around the new product cycle catalyst. The technical breakout (score 84.4) confirms momentum despite a death cross, while implied volatility remains elevated as options markets price ongoing headline risk from the competitive CPU landscape. What stands out is the structural P/C divergence — index puts heavy at 1.39 while single-names show call preference at 0.89 — indicating sophisticated dispersion positioning ahead of Core PCE and next week's NFP. This setup intensifies under SPY's $-19.9B negative gamma, where dealer hedging amplifies any directional moves. The combination of institutional put protection on indices and single-name call exposure creates maximum sensitivity to economic surprises. Microsoft's massive May call spread (70K contracts across 405/445 strikes) aligns with Millennium's aggressive Q4 build ($2.49B added), but occurs against severely oversold technicals (RSI 26, score 11.8). The $0.50 premium on 445C suggests positioning for a major recovery rally, potentially catalyzed by AI monetization themes that could emerge if Core PCE shows continued disinflation progress.

Layer 1: Sentiment -21Layer 2: GEX -$19.9BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.39/0.89Layer 8: ARM CPU news
